{
  "current_goal": "Refine the static page's skip-to-content keyboard navigation with minimal accessibility-only changes.",
  "completed": [
    "Confirmed the page already had a skip link as the first focusable element.",
    "Made the main content target programmatically focusable for reliable skip-link behavior.",
    "Added concise CSS so the focused main region is visibly indicated after skipping."
  ],
  "key_files": [
    "index.html",
    "styles.css"
  ],
  "latest_verification": [
    "`index.html` contains `<main id=\"main-content\" tabindex=\"-1\">`.",
    "`styles.css` contains `main:focus-visible` outline styling.",
    "Existing skip-link markup remains in place as the first focusable element in `body`."
  ],
  "open_questions": [],
  "risks": [],
  "suggested_next_step": "Run a quick browser keyboard pass to confirm the skip link reveals on focus and jumps to the main content as expected.",
  "web_recovery_prompt": "Open the updated static page and verify keyboard navigation. Check that the first Tab focuses the existing \"Skip to content\" link, pressing Enter moves focus to `#main-content`, a visible outline appears on the main region, and no visual/layout regressions were introduced."
}
